name: Côte d'Ivoire

colors.symbolism: the savannas of the north; national development and the dynamic growth of the country
peace and the unity of all citizens
hope and the forests of the south; agricultural wealth

description: Three equal vertical stripes — orange at the hoist, white in the centre, green at the fly. The arrangement is the mirror image of the flag of Ireland (which is green-white-orange).
history: Adopted on 3 December 1959, ahead of independence from France on 7 August 1960, and inspired by the French tricolour. The colours echo the Pan-African green-yellow-red palette but are adapted to the country's own symbolism. Côte d'Ivoire's flag and Ireland's flag use the same three colours in opposite order, which has occasionally caused diplomatic confusion.

features.symbolism: orange for the savannas of the north and the country's progress, white for peace and unity, green for hope and the forests of the south
